title: Reduced final-stop-window entry as a monotonic timing signal
summary: Test whether pit probability rises when a fresh compound first becomes able to reach the finish, but implement the idea with a minimal low-alias feature set.
rationale: Pit probability should plausibly rise when a fresh compound first becomes able to reach the finish, but broad versions of this idea can add too many aliases and brittle threshold interactions. A reduced monotonic representation may preserve the strategic signal with less overfit.
implementation_hint: Add a minimal final-stop-window-entry block to the step-65 feature set: per-compound entry margins such as FreshSoftEntryMargin, FreshMediumEntryMargin, and FreshHardEntryMargin, plus AnyFreshEntryNow and EntryCount. Avoid softest-opening aliases, many boolean thresholds, and high-order interactions. First test only EntryCount and AnyFreshEntryNow.
expected_effect: Small to medium potential improvement if it improves ranking near final-stop windows. Confidence is low to medium because this feature family has high CV-only overfit risk.
risk: High risk of CV-only overfit. A tiny CV gain without grouped-CV confirmation should be treated as suspicious. Grouped validation is required before submission.
